preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar lateral fricative [ɬ] to the voiced alveolar lateral approximant [l] is a process of de-fricativization and voicing (lenition). This change frequently occurs in loanword adaptation or as part of consonant mutation systems where complex lateral sounds simplify into the more common liquid [l].

examples.note: Adaptation of the Welsh lateral fricative into the English lateral approximant.
Grammaticalized shift from voiceless fricative to voiced approximant.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Ball, M. J., & Muller, N. (1992). Mutation in Welsh.
